title: Pre-op frailty anesthesia review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

assessment.text: Supports documenting frailty status for older or at-risk surgical patients, comprehensive geriatric assessment when performed, and cognition assessment.
assessment.rationale: The exact CPOC/BGS standards support documenting frailty status, comprehensive geriatric assessment, and cognition when assessed, without inferring frailty.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports documenting frailty status for older or at-risk surgical patients, comprehensive geriatric assessment when performed, and cognition assessment.

source.title: Guideline for Perioperative Care for People Living with Frailty Undergoing Elective and Emergency Surgery

exact_section.heading: Standards 3-5 - frailty, comprehensive geriatric assessment, and cognition

referral.text: Supports documenting frailty score, nutrition and BMI, comorbidities, cognition, medicines, functional status, reassessment, and individualized shared decisions.
referral.rationale: The exact referral and preassessment section supports documenting interval change, function, comorbidity, medicines, nutrition, cognition, patient priorities, and an individualized clinician-entered discussion.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting frailty score, nutrition and BMI, comorbidities, cognition, medicines, functional status, reassessment, and individualized shared decisions.

source.title: Guideline for Perioperative Care for People Living with Frailty Undergoing Elective and Emergency Surgery

exact_section.heading: Recommendations for primary care, surgical outpatients, and preoperative assessment

source_limitations: Frailty instrument and score, cognition result, function, nutrition, multimorbidity, medicines, social support, capacity, goals, risk estimate, comprehensive assessment, optimization, discharge, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
